options.why: Friday has the shortest bar, so it is the least attended night, not the most.
Thursday ($40$) is second, but Tuesday's bar ($50$) is taller.
Monday's bar is short; it was not the busiest night.

questions.solution_text: Tuesday has the tallest bar, so the most people came on Tuesday.

options.why: Added the two values ($15+12$) instead of subtracting them.
Read only Ben's goals and forgot to subtract Dan's.
Read Dan's goals instead of finding the difference.

options.text: Numbers are level on Monday and Tuesday, then rise each day.

options.why: The points climb from Wednesday on; none is lower than the one before.

options.text: Numbers stay the same all week.
options.why: Only Monday and Tuesday are equal; after that each point is higher.
No point is lower than the one before it, so there is no fall.

questions.solution_text: Visitor numbers are level on Monday and Tuesday, then rise each day, so overall attendance is increasing.

options.why: Repeated Friday's value instead of continuing the daily rise.
Added $300$ (two steps) instead of one daily step of $150$.
Added half a step ($75$) rather than the full daily rise of $150$.

questions.solution_text:
From Tuesday the visitors rise by $150$ each day.
$650+150$
$=800$

options.text: The battery stayed full for an hour, then fell by an equal amount each hour.
The battery fell by the same amount the whole time.
options.why: It was flat for the first hour, so it did not fall the whole time.
The readings drop from $100\%$ to $40\%$, so the battery fell, not rose.

options.text: The battery fell by more each hour.
options.why: Each drop is the same, $20\%$; the falls are equal, not growing.

questions.solution_text:
The battery stays at $100\%$ for the first hour, then falls by the same amount, $20\%$, each hour: $100$ to $80$ to $60$ to $40$.
So it held steady, then fell by equal steps.
